skill.name: qwen-image-2
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Qwen-Image-2.0 (fast), Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro (professional text rendering). Capabilities: text-to-image, multi-image editing, complex text rendering. Triggers: qwen image, qwen-image, alibaba image, dashscope image, qwen image 2, qwen image pro
skill.summary: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: All 53 static analysis findings are false positives. The detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns are markdown code fences in documentation, not executable code. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate service and documentation links for inference.sh integration. No actual security risks present - this is a documentation-only skill providing CLI usage examples.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Flagged
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 8 hardcoded URLs as potential network risks. These are legitimate documentation and service URLs for inference.sh integration, not exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to legitimate inference.sh documentation and example.com placeholders in code samples - no evidence of malicious data transfer

content.user_title: Generate Images with Alibaba Qwen AI
content.value_statement: Create AI-generated images and edit existing photos using Alibaba's Qwen-Image-2.0 models through Claude Code. Generate artwork, product photos, posters, and complex text overlays directly from natural language descriptions.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text descriptions using Qwen-Image-2.0 and Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro models
Edit existing images by providing reference images and modification instructions
Create multi-image compositions combining elements from up to 3 reference images
Generate images with custom dimensions from 512x512 to 2048x2048 pixels
Render complex text in images with professional quality using the Pro model
Control output randomness with seed values for reproducible results
limitations: Requires external inference.sh CLI installation and account setup
Image generation requires network connectivity to inference.sh API
Maximum prompt length is 800 characters, negative prompt max 500 characters
Output resolution limited to 2048x2048 pixels total
Cannot generate images without active inference.sh API access
Text rendering quality varies between standard and Pro models

use_cases.title: Generate Marketing Visuals
use_cases.description: Create product photos, advertisements, and marketing materials for e-commerce or social media campaigns using text descriptions

use_cases.title: Design Posters with Text
use_cases.description: Generate posters, flyers, and announcements with accurate text rendering using the Pro model for professional text-heavy designs

use_cases.title: Create Concept Art
use_cases.description: Generate concept art, storyboards, and visual references for creative projects using various styles like photorealistic, illustration, or digital art
use_cases.target_user: Artists, game developers, and creative teams

prompt_templates.title: Generate Basic Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [description] using Qwen-Image-2.0
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple text-to-image generation for quick visual concepts

prompt_templates.title: Create Poster with Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a poster using Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro with title [text] at the top in [color] and subtitle [text] below. Background should show [description]
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating text-heavy designs like posters, flyers, or announcements

prompt_templates.title: Edit Multiple Reference Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Using Qwen-Image-2.0, edit the image from [source] by combining [element 1] from reference image 1 and [element 2] from reference image 2
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-image editing to combine elements from different source images

prompt_templates.title: Generate Custom Resolution
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a [width]x[height] image of [description] using Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro with negative prompt [undesired elements]
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating images with specific dimensions while avoiding certain visual elements

output_examples.input: Generate a futuristic cityscape at sunset with flying cars
output_examples.output: Creates a photorealistic image showing a city skyline with orange and purple sunset sky, illuminated skyscrapers, and flying vehicles throughout the scene

output_examples.input: Create a 1024x1536 poster with title Summer Sale in bold red text, 50% Off in blue, beach background
output_examples.output: Generates a vertical poster image with crisp red title text, blue subtitle text, and a tropical beach scene with palm trees and ocean

output_examples.input: Make the person from image1 wear the outfit from image2 in the pose from image3
output_examples.output: Produces a composite image combining facial features from the first reference, clothing from the second, and body positioning from the third reference image

best_practices: Use the Pro model for any design requiring text, logos, or typography as it renders text significantly better than the standard model
Set prompt_extend to false when you need precise control over the generated output and want to prevent automatic prompt enhancement
Start with the standard Qwen-Image-2.0 model for faster iteration, then switch to Pro only when you need higher quality or text rendering
Use negative prompts to exclude unwanted elements like low quality, blurry features, or specific objects you do not want in the output
Set a seed value when you find a good result so you can reproduce the same image or make variations from a known starting point
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill without first installing the inference.sh CLI as it requires the external infsh command to function
Avoid exceeding the 800 character limit on prompts or 500 character limit on negative prompts as the generation will fail
Do not use the standard model for complex text rendering as it often produces garbled or incorrect text characters
Avoid requesting total pixel dimensions outside the 512x512 to 2048x2048 range as the API will reject the request
Do not reference local file paths directly in reference_images without uploading them to accessible URLs first

faq.question: What is the difference between Qwen-Image-2.0 and Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro?
faq.answer: The standard Qwen-Image-2.0 model is faster and suitable for general image generation. The Pro model provides professional-grade text rendering, enhanced semantic understanding, and better realism, making it ideal for posters, logos, and text-heavy designs.

faq.question: Do I need an API key or account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the inference.sh CLI tool and log in with your account. Run 'infsh login' to authenticate before using the image generation commands.

faq.question: What image resolutions are supported?
faq.answer: You can generate images with width and height between 512 and 2048 pixels, but the total pixel count must stay between 512x512 and 2048x2048. Common sizes like 1024x1024, 1536x1024, and 1024x1536 are supported.

faq.question: How do I edit existing images?
faq.answer: Provide 1 to 3 reference images as URLs in the reference_images array, then describe what you want to change or combine. The model will use your reference images as input and modify them based on your prompt.

faq.question: Why does text appear garbled in my generated images?
faq.answer: The standard Qwen-Image-2.0 model has limited text rendering capabilities. Switch to the Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro model for accurate text rendering, and consider setting prompt_extend to false for precise control.

faq.question: Can I reproduce the same image multiple times?
faq.answer: Yes, use the seed parameter with any integer value between 0 and 2147483647. Using the same seed with identical prompt and parameters will produce the same image, allowing for reproducible results.
